creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Anthropic’s new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.

title: (No AI) Something’s off with Midjourney’s pivot to body scanners
author: Robert Hart
content:
![Midjourney Medical 3D scans of a “phantom” body](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/ultrasound_phantom_segmentation-b254a71d083eb5c9.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A scan of an imaging phantom, segmented to validate how cleanly structures separate under controlled conditions. | Image: Midjourney Medical

Last week, Midjourney, an AI startup best known for its image generator, [made an unusual pivot](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/952011/midjourney-medical-ai-ultrasound-scan): medical imaging.

The company [announced](https://www.midjourney.com/medical/blogpost) a futuristic ultrasound scanner that would dunk users into a vat of water and, hopefully, produce "something as powerful as MRI" yet "as casual as a trip to the spa." Midjourney says the goal is to help people live longer, better, and healthier lives. CEO David Holz has [suggested](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/952011/midjourney-medical-ai-ultrasound-scan) the system could one day be better than MRI. Experts are skeptical. While several medical imaging specialists told _The Verge_ they were not dismissive of the idea outright, they said Midjourney has shown little public evidence to su …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/report/954826/midjourney-medical-ai-ultrasound-body-scanner-lacks-evidence)

title: (No AI) How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
content: GPT-5 Pro helped solve a 3-year-old immunology mystery, offering insights into T cell behavior. The breakthrough could support cancer and autoimmune research.
